<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

```yaml
# =============================================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY — Food Delivery Platform
# Polyglot microservices: Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway · Java/Spring Boot ·
# Python/FastAPI · Go/net/http · TypeScript/amqplib
# =============================================================================
# HOW TO USE:
#   - The agent updates this file at the end of every turn.
#   - Human reviewers should read `open_questions` before continuing a session.
#   - Fields marked REQUIRED must be populated before any code is written.
#   - Fields marked APPEND-ONLY must never have existing entries removed.
# =============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. SESSION STATE
# What is happening right now, at what granularity, and are we safe to proceed?
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:

  task:
    id: ""                          # REQUIRED. Short slug, e.g. "add-eta-endpoint"
    title: ""                       # REQUIRED. One-line human-readable description
    description: ""                 # Full description of the work being done
    ticket_or_issue_ref: ""         # GitHub issue / Jira ticket URL if available

  status:
    value: "not_started"            # REQUIRED. Enum: not_started | in_progress |
                                    #   blocked | awaiting_review | complete
    updated_at: ""                  # ISO-8601 timestamp of last status change
    blocked_reason: ""              # Populated only when status == blocked

  phase:
    current: ""                     # REQUIRED. Enum: planning | scaffolding |
                                    #   implementation | testing | refactoring |
                                    #   review | done
    next: ""                        # What phase comes after completing current work

  scope:
    services_in_scope: []           # Subset of: [api-gateway, order-service,
                                    #   inventory-service, driver-service,
                                    #   notification-service]
    services_read_only: []          # Services consulted but not modified
    cross_service_event_changes: false  # true if RabbitMQ exchange/routing keys
                                        # are touched — requires coordinating ALL
                                        # consumers before committing

  safety_checks:
    openapi_yaml_modified: false    # Must stay false unless explicitly instructed
    env_files_touched: false        # .env / .env.example — must stay false
    docker_compose_touched: false   # docker-compose.yml — must stay false
    ci_workflows_touched: false     # .github/workflows/* — must stay false
    lock_files_touched: false       # go.sum / package-lock.json — must stay false
    state_machine_weakened: false   # OrderStatus.isValidTransition — must stay false
    stripe_integration_disabled: false  # Must stay false

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DECISIONS MADE
# Append-only log of WHY each non-trivial technical choice was made.
# This prevents re-litigating settled questions across turns.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made:  # APPEND-ONLY — never remove an entry

  - id: ""                          # REQUIRED. Sequential integer as string "001"
    timestamp: ""                   # ISO-8601
    service: ""                     # Which service this decision belongs to;
                                    #   one of the five microservices or "cross-cutting"
    layer: ""                       # Enum: domain | persistence | api | messaging |
                                    #       auth | payment | infra | test
    title: ""                       # Short title, e.g. "Use Redis SETNX for lock"
    context: ""                     # What problem or constraint triggered this decision
    decision: ""                    # What was decided
    rationale: ""                   # WHY — link to domain_concepts, pitfalls, etc.
    alternatives_rejected: []       # List of strings — options considered and discarded
    consequences: ""                # Side-effects or follow-up work created
    reversible: true                # false if hard to undo (e.g., DB schema change)
    linked_files: []                # Paths of files most affected by this decision

  # ── Example (delete when real entries exist) ──────────────────────────────
  # - id: "001"
  #   timestamp: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  #   service: "order-service"
  #   layer: "payment"
  #   title: "Keep Stripe CaptureMethod.MANUAL for order acceptance flow"
  #   context: >
  #     Order acceptance happens asynchronously after inventory reservation.
  #     Immediate capture would charge customers before stock is confirmed.
  #   decision: "Retain manual capture; capture only after ACCEPTED transition."
  #   rationale: >
  #     Matches existing OrderService pattern (stripePaymentIntentId stored on
  #     Order entity). Aligns with pitfall: 'payment intents must be explicitly
  #     captured after order acceptance'.
  #   alternatives_rejected:
  #     - "Automatic capture: risks charging for unfulfillable orders"
  #     - "Capture at PREPARING: too late, increases chargeback window"
  #   consequences: "ACCEPTED transition handler must call stripe.capture()."
  #   reversible: false
  #   linked_files:
  #     - "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
  #     - "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FILES MODIFIED
# Track every file touched in this session with enough context to understand
# the change without re-reading the diff.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified:  # APPEND-ONLY — never remove an entry

  - path: ""                        # REQUIRED. Repo-relative path
    service: ""                     # Owning microservice
    language: ""                    # Enum: kotlin | java | python | go | typescript
    build_tool: ""                  # Enum: maven | gradle | pip | go | npm
    change_type: ""                 # Enum: created | modified | deleted | renamed
    reason: ""                      # REQUIRED. Why was this file changed?
    change_summary: ""              # What was actually changed (not the diff itself)
    domain_concepts_affected: []    # Names from domain_concepts list, e.g. ["OrderStatus"]
    key_components_affected: []     # Names from key_components list
    touches_state_machine: false    # true if OrderStatus transitions were modified
    touches_payment: false          # true if Stripe logic was modified
    touches_rabbitmq: false         # true if exchange/routing/binding logic changed
    touches_auth: false             # true if JWT / JwtAuthFilter / X-Customer-Id changed
    touches_redis_lock: false       # true if acquire_lock / Redis lock logic changed
    migration_required: false       # true if a DB migration script is now needed
    test_file: false                # true if this IS a test file
    paired_test_file: ""            # Path to the corresponding test file, if any
    reviewed: false                 # Set to true after human sign-off

  # ── Restricted path guard (copy this block for any write near restricted paths)
  # Before writing, confirm:
  #   path NOT IN [.env, .env.example, docker-compose.yml, driver-service/go.sum,
  #                notification-service/package-lock.json, openapi.yaml,
  #                .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/agentic-ready.yml,
  #                .github/workflows/context-drift-detector.yml]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. TESTS RUN
# Log of every test execution during this session, per service / toolchain.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run:  # APPEND-ONLY — never remove an entry

  - id: ""                          # Sequential integer as string "001"
    timestamp: ""                   # ISO-8601
    service: ""                     # Which microservice was tested
    framework: ""                   # Enum: junit | pytest | go_test | jest
    command: ""                     # Exact command executed, e.g.
                                    #   "cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress"
    scope: ""                       # Enum: unit | integration | contract | e2e | full
    triggered_by: ""                # Why tests were run, e.g. "after adding ETA endpoint"

    result:
      status: ""                    # Enum: passed | failed | error | skipped
      total: 0                      # Total test count
      passed: 0
      failed: 0
      skipped: 0
      duration_seconds: 0.0
      failure_summary: ""           # Short description of failures if status != passed

    coverage:
      reported: false               # true if coverage metrics were emitted
      line_pct: null                # e.g. 82.4 — null if not reported
      branch_pct: null
      critical_paths_covered: []    # List of business-critical paths verified,
                                    #   e.g. ["OrderStatus.isValidTransition",
                                    #          "acquire_lock race condition"]

    new_tests_added: []             # Paths of test files created or meaningfully
                                    #   extended in this session

    regressions_detected: false     # true if a previously-passing test now fails
    regression_detail: ""           # Populated if regressions_detected == true

  # ── Per-service test command reference ─────────────────────────────────────
  # order-service:      cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress
  # api-gateway:        cd api-gateway && ./gradlew test
  # inventory-service:  cd inventory-service && pytest
  # driver-service:     cd driver-service && go test ./...
  # notification-service: cd notification-service && npm test

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. OPEN QUESTIONS
# Items that block progress or require human judgment before the agent proceeds.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
open_questions:  # APPEND-ONLY — never remove an entry; mark resolved instead

  - id: ""                          # Sequential integer as string "001"
    timestamp: ""                   # ISO-8601 when question was raised
    raised_by: "agent"              # Enum: agent | human
    service: ""                     # Affected microservice, or "cross-cutting"
    priority: ""                    # Enum: blocking | high | medium | low
    question: ""                    # REQUIRED. The precise question

    context: ""                     # Why this question matters; include any
                                    #   relevant domain_concepts or pitfall names

    options: []                     # Candidate answers the agent has identified

    default_if_unresolved: ""       # What the agent will do if human doesn't respond;
                                    #   leave empty if no safe default exists

    resolution:
      status: "open"                # Enum: open | resolved | wont_fix
      answer: ""                    # Human's answer
      resolved_at: ""               # ISO-8601
      decision_ref: ""              # ID of the decisions_made entry that captures
                                    #   the follow-up action

  # ── Questions that should always be asked before certain changes ───────────
  # RabbitMQ exchange change:  "Have all consumers (notification-service) been
  #   updated to match the new exchange name / routing key?"
  # New OrderStatus state:     "Has isValidTransition been updated and all
  #   downstream consumers (Notification Service event handlers) been informed?"
  # New env variable:          "Has the variable been added to both .env.example
  #   AND the docker-compose.yml environment block for the correct service?"
  # Stripe flow change:        "Does the change preserve manual capture semantics
  #   (CaptureMethod.MANUAL) required by the order acceptance flow?"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. DOMAIN STATE
# Runtime knowledge about the Food Delivery Platform's domain and framework
# state that must persist across turns to avoid contradictions.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
domain_state:

  # ── Order Lifecycle ─
